# Checklist: CSV To Planner Import Workflow Requirements Quality

**Purpose**: Validate that the CSV import workflow specification is complete, unambiguous, consistent, measurable, and ready for implementation.
**Created**: 2026-05-11
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are the core user journeys for validation, preview, confirmation, execution, and reporting all covered with explicit requirements in FR-002 through FR-009? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002-FR-009]
- [ ] CHK002 Are all CSV-input constraints documented, including file type, size limit, required fields, empty files, and extra-column handling? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, FR-002, FR-012, FR-013]
- [ ] CHK003 Are all execution outcomes covered, including created, skipped existing, partial failure, retry-once failure, and manual follow-up actions? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007a-FR-008c, FR-017]
- [ ] CHK004 Are both runtime modes and their mode-entry differences specified well enough to support implementation and testing? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011, FR-018, NFR-005, NFR-008]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK005 Is "clear validation" defined with specific user-visible output expectations rather than subjective wording? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002-FR-004]
- [ ] CHK006 Is the matching rule "task name only" explicit enough about case sensitivity, trimming, and duplicate-name ambiguity? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-007b, FR-007a]
- [ ] CHK007 Is the fixed `General` bucket fallback described clearly enough to show when it applies and whether it is configurable? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014, Assumption]
- [ ] CHK008 Are priority token mappings and the invalid-value rule stated in a way that leaves no ambiguity for operators and non-UI callers? [Clarity, Spec §FR-015]
- [ ] CHK009 Is the stale-preview rule clear about which state changes invalidate execution and how "fresh preview" is re-established? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008c, FR-016]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK010 Do the preview, execution, and reporting requirements use consistent terminology for the same import item across FR-004, FR-007, and FR-008? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004, FR-007, FR-008]
- [ ] CHK011 Are the partial-success rules consistent between FR-008a, the user story acceptance scenarios, and the execution report requirements? [Consistency, Spec §FR-008a, User Story 2, FR-008]
- [ ] CHK012 Are the runtime-mode statements in FR-011, FR-018, NFR-005, and NFR-008 aligned rather than duplicative or contradictory? [Consistency, Spec §FR-011, FR-018, NFR-005, NFR-008]
- [ ] CHK013 Is the secret-masking requirement aligned with the execution-report and error-handling requirements so that all user-facing output follows the same safety rule? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, SC-006]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK014 Are the success criteria measurable enough to judge preview performance, confirmation blocking, and report completeness without interpretation? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001-SC-007]
- [ ] CHK015 Is the 95% under 10 seconds preview target defined with enough measurement context to make it reproducible? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001, NFR-004]
- [ ] CHK016 Are the "100% blocked" confirmation and no-secret requirements stated as testable acceptance conditions rather than general goals? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002, SC-006]
- [ ] CHK017 Are the automated test expectations specific enough to show which behaviours need unit, integration, regression, and UI coverage? [Clarity, Spec §NFR-002, tasks.md]
- [ ] CHK018 Does the spec define what evidence must be captured for performance and CI/AppHost validation in the implementation notes? [Completeness, Spec §NFR-004, NFR-007, tasks.md]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK019 Are the primary, alternate, exception, recovery, and non-functional scenarios all represented in the requirements set? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios, Edge Cases, NFRs]
- [ ] CHK020 Are zero-row, header-only, duplicate-row, and missing-required-column CSV cases explicitly covered? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, FR-002, FR-003]
- [ ] CHK021 Are partial-failure and interrupted-execution scenarios covered so the report requirements remain truthful under failure? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, FR-008a, FR-008b]
- [ ] CHK022 Are the stale-preview and destination selection-change scenarios covered both as an execution blocker and as a user-facing explanation? [Coverage, Spec §FR-008c, FR-016, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK023 Are mode-specific entry and authentication flows covered for both Graph and in-memory runtime modes? [Coverage, Spec §FR-011, FR-018, NFR-005, NFR-008]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK024 Are the requirements explicit about what happens when extra CSV columns are present, including the UI default and the non-UI parser contract? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK025 Are the requirements explicit about what happens when a CSV row provides no bucket, and is the fixed default behaviour treated as intentional rather than incidental? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-014, Assumption]
- [ ] CHK026 Are the retry-once transient Graph failure and the "continue remaining rows" rules detailed enough to prevent ambiguity about per-row and whole-run outcomes? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-008a, FR-008b]
- [ ] CHK027 Are manual action requirements explicit about which actions are emitted for created versus already-existing tasks? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-017]
- [ ] CHK028 Are file-size rejection and parser-validation boundaries defined so oversized files fail before parsing and smaller invalid files fail at row/file level as intended? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-012, FR-002, FR-003]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK029 Are the external dependency expectations for Microsoft Graph and runtime authentication documented as assumptions rather than implied behaviour? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, FR-018]
- [ ] CHK030 Are the single-tenant scope and available planner destination assumptions explicit and aligned with the planned implementation scope? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, NFR-006]
- [ ] CHK031 Are the testing and CI/AppHost dependencies explicit enough that contributors know which validation steps are mandatory for this feature? [Dependencies, Spec §NFR-002, NFR-007, tasks.md]
- [ ] CHK032 Are the operator knowledge assumptions, such as CSV conventions and awareness of the `General` bucket fallback, documented where they affect user-facing behaviour? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, FR-014]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK033 Are there any hidden conflicts between the spec's "single-use utility" framing and the multi-step validation/confirmation/reporting workflow that should be called out explicitly? [Conflict, Spec §Input, User Scenarios]
- [ ] CHK034 Is the distinction between preview-time fingerprints and execution-time freshness checks described without relying on implementation details that the spec does not define? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-016, FR-008c]
- [ ] CHK035 Does the spec clearly distinguish user-safe error messaging from internal diagnostic detail so implementers know what is intentionally omitted? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-010, SC-006]
- [ ] CHK036 Are the responsibilities of the application layer, gateway layer, and UI layer described clearly enough to avoid scope creep or duplicate rules? [Conflict, Spec §NFR-001, plan.md]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
